examples.text: أُنَاشِدُ وَزِيرَ البِتْرُولِ حَلَّ مَشَاكِلِ مَصَانِعِ طُوبِ زِفْتَى
examples.english: I appeal to the minster of petrol to solve the problems of the brick factories of Zefta

etymology.text: From Coptic ⲧⲱⲃⲉ (tōbe, “brick”), from Demotic Egyptian tb (“brick”), from Egyptian ḏbt (“brick, block, ingot”): Db-b-t:O39 potentially related to Sumerian 𒁾 (dub).

body.text: : (S, O, Msb, K:) n. un. with

: so says Az, and El-Fárábee says the like; and accord. to them it seems to be [genuine] Arabic: accord. to IDrd, it is of the dial. of Syria; but [Fei adds] I think it to be of the dial. of Room: (Msb:) or it is of the dial. of Egypt: (S, O:) [probably of Coptic origin. It is mentioned in the S and K in art.
, mentioned in this art. in the S and K, [as though it were originally
